title: Verifier Report Presentation Registry - v0
description: Presentation layer over claim_registry_v0.json. Maps engine claim names to human-facing report lines (dimension, label per verdict, provenance, forbidden wording). This artifact governs ONLY how already-computed verdicts are rendered. It does NOT define, alter, or contribute to verification semantics, and MUST never influence a verdict, an exit code, or the JSON output contract. It is intentionally not part of the pinned trust/semantics artifact set.
invariants: Every claim in claim_registry_v0.json appears exactly once across `assertions[].claim` or `not_surfaced`.
Every `assertions[].labels.supported` (the assurance label) must be weaker than or exactly equivalent to the referenced claim's `assertion` in claim_registry_v0.json. Never stronger.
Failure/risk labels (disproved) must be at least as severe as the underlying reason. Never softer.
No label may contain any phrase in `global_forbidden_wording` or the entry's `forbidden_wording`.
Every rendered line carries exactly one provenance class.
The presentation layer can reorder, group, and relabel; it can never derive a new verification conclusion.
_integrity_claims_doc: Claims whose `disproved` verdict means the evidence itself was forged or altered (a signature, hash, or chain-continuity failure). A required disproved integrity claim drives the Evidence line to TAMPERED; any other required disproved (non-violation) claim drives it to INVALID. Violation claims (violation_on_disproved) are excluded here and surface in the Finding line instead.

dimensions.title: Authenticity
dimensions.question: Is this evidence real and unmodified?

dimensions.title: Authorization
dimensions.question: What did the permit authorize, and who approved it?

dimensions.title: Execution
dimensions.question: Did the recorded action match the permit?

dimensions.title: Revocation
dimensions.question: Was authority later withdrawn, and did anything run after?

dimensions.title: Trust
dimensions.question: What anchored the signing keys to a trusted root?

dimensions.title: Completeness
dimensions.question: Was the evidence complete and in declared scope?

trust_modes.label: Self-attested (embedded key only)
trust_modes.warning: Proves internal consistency only. Does not prove Keel signed this artifact.

trust_modes.label: Untrusted signer
trust_modes.warning: Signature math is valid, but the signing key is not anchored to any accepted trust root.

_artifact_lines_doc: Signed permit fields surfaced by the renderer from artifact.permit (lifted from the verified canonical payload). Rendered only when the permit's signature verified.

header_reduction.note: Deterministic projection of claims[] (and session/artifact lines) onto two header lines. Implemented in the renderer; encoded here so renderers stay consistent. `required` per-claim comes from the VerificationReport, not from this file.

precedence.when: any REQUIRED claim verdict == disproved (authenticity/integrity family)
any other REQUIRED non-violation claim verdict == disproved
report.error is present and claims[] is empty

finding_line.note: Describes what the (trusted) evidence says. VIOLATION takes precedence and must never be hidden by a passing Evidence line.
violation_when_any: decision == DENY and an execution/dispatch record is present

next_step_rules.note: Next Step is mechanical and attaches ONLY to evidence/session deficiencies. Findings (e.g. violations) are stated as fact and carry no advice.

rules.next_step: Re-run without --self-attested to verify against the Keel trust root.
Provide the timestamp receipt to enable timestamp verification.
Provide the missing evidence listed under the incomplete checks.
Upgrade keel-verifier; one or more claims are newer than this build understands.

labels.supported: Export contents unchanged; manifest signature valid
labels.disproved: Export contents or manifest signature INVALID
labels.unverifiable_scope: Export integrity: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Evidence bundle structure and signature valid
labels.unverifiable_scope: Evidence bundle: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.disproved: Audit trail BROKEN (chain discontinuity or reorder)
labels.unverifiable_scope: Audit trail: not checkable by this verifier version
Checkpoint contents: not checkable by this verifier version
Checkpoint signature: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.disproved: Trusted timestamp does NOT match the checkpoint
labels.unverifiable_scope: Trusted timestamp: not checkable by this verifier version
External timestamp chain: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Permit decision signed by the issuing key (issuance-time)
labels.unverifiable_scope: Permit decision: not checkable by this verifier version
Operator approval: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Operator approval verified (key in good standing at signing)
labels.unverifiable_scope: Operator approval: not checkable by this verifier version
Operator approval: not checkable by this verifier version
Buyer counter-signature: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Buyer counter-signature verified (key in good standing at signing)
labels.unverifiable_scope: Buyer counter-signature: not checkable by this verifier version
Human passkey co-signature: not checkable by this verifier version
Buyer counter-signature: not checkable by this verifier version
Audit attestation: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Audit attestation verified (key in good standing at signing)
labels.unverifiable_scope: Audit attestation: not checkable by this verifier version
Audit attestation: not checkable by this verifier version
Completion record: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Recorded response matches the evidence digests
labels.disproved: Recorded response does NOT match the evidence digests
labels.unverifiable_scope: Recorded response: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.disproved: Dispatch did NOT match the permit
labels.unverifiable_scope: Dispatch binding: not checkable by this verifier version
Revocation event: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: No dispatch after revocation, within the verified scope
labels.unverifiable_scope: Post-revocation dispatch absence: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Authority chain valid and narrowed at each step
labels.unverifiable_scope: Authority chain: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Signing keys were in service when they signed
labels.disproved: A signing key was revoked or compromised before it signed
labels.unverifiable_scope: Signing-key timing: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Export is a faithful slice of the recorded chain
labels.disproved: Export is NOT a faithful slice of the recorded chain
labels.unverifiable_scope: Scope faithfulness: not checkable by this verifier version

labels.supported: Key-status evidence complete up to the checkpoint boundary
labels.unverifiable_scope: Key-status evidence: not checkable by this verifier version

not_surfaced.reason: Identity metadata; shown in --details, not a headline assurance.
Specialized negative-case adjudication; detail view only.
Workflow-specific; surfaced only for workflow artifacts in --details.
Sidecar internal to scope-faithfulness/key-status; rolled up under Completeness.
Specialized authority-root key-status timing claim; detail view only.
Specialized authority-root terminal-disable timing claim; detail view only.
Specialized authority-edge key-status timing claim; detail view only.
Specialized rail settlement-reconciliation claim; detail view only.
Budget ledger claim; detail view only (not part of the permit identity/action view).
Quota reservation linkage claim; detail view only.
